clusters.title: Attack in Strait of Hormuz halts evacuation plan for stranded ships
clusters.neutral_headline: UN agency pauses ship evacuations through strait of Hormuz after vessel struck
bullet_summary: The ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman but no casualties were reported
Reported by Financial Times, France24, Guardian World and others

items.title: Attack in Strait of Hormuz halts evacuation plan for stranded ships
items.standfirst: International Maritime Organization announces pause after container ship was hit by ‘unknown projectile’
items.content: International Maritime Organization announces pause after container ship was hit by ‘unknown projectile’

items.title: US seeks Iran deal, but 'not at any price,' Rubio says
items.standfirst: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Thursday Washington would not accept any Iran deal allowing Tehran to impose fees on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, warning it would cause "total chaos." His remarks came after an attack on a vessel forced the United Nations to suspend mariner evacuation efforts.
items.content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Thursday Washington would not accept any Iran deal allowing Tehran to impose fees on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, warning it would cause "total chaos." His remarks came after an attack on a vessel forced the United Nations to suspend mariner evacuation efforts.

items.title: UN agency pauses ship evacuations through strait of Hormuz after vessel struck
items.standfirst: International Maritime Organization says safety guarantees must be confirmed before ships can move againA United Nations agency has paused the evacuation of ships through the strait of Hormuz after the British military said a vessel was hit by a projectile off the coast of Oman following the passage of several tankers that used a route backed by the UN.The head of the UN’s International Maritime Organization said on Thursday that the plan to move stranded ships out of the Persian Gulf through the strait would be on hold until the agency could confirm safety guarantees for the ships on the evacuation list and in the region. Continue reading...
items.content: International Maritime Organization says safety guarantees must be confirmed before ships can move againA United Nations agency has paused the evacuation of ships through the strait of Hormuz after the British military said a vessel was hit by a projectile off the coast of Oman following the passage of several tankers that used a route backed by the UN.The head of the UN’s International Maritime Organization said on Thursday that the plan to move stranded ships out of the Persian Gulf through the strait would be on hold until the agency could confirm safety guarantees for the ships on the evacuation list and in the region. Continue reading...

items.title: UN agency pauses Hormuz ship evacuation initiative after vessel attacked
items.standfirst: Iran warns vessels to use only Tehran-approved routes as IMO halts Hormuz evacuation after ship struck.
items.content: Iran warns vessels to use only Tehran-approved routes as IMO halts Hormuz evacuation after ship struck.

items.content: The ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman but no casualties were reported.

items.title: UN suspends Strait of Hormuz evacuation after ship 'hit by projectile'
items.standfirst: The UN has halted the evacuation of thousands of seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel came under a suspected attack.
items.content: The UN has halted the evacuation of thousands of seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel came under a suspected attack.

items.title: U.N. agency pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation effort as ship struck off Oman
items.standfirst: The United Nations’ shipping agency on Thursday said it would pause an evacuation effort ​to get hundreds of stranded ships out through the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel was attacked ​in the Gulf of Oman.“I have ​been informed of an attack today ⁠in the Gulf of Oman on ​a vessel which passed through the Strait ​of Hormuz,” Arsenio Dominguez, Secretary-General of the ​U
items.content: The United Nations’ shipping agency on Thursday said it would pause an evacuation effort ​to get hundreds of stranded ships out through the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel was attacked ​in the Gulf of Oman.“I have ​been informed of an attack today ⁠in the Gulf of Oman on ​a vessel which passed through the Strait ​of Hormuz,” Arsenio Dominguez, Secretary-General of the ​U

clusters.title: Supreme court hands Trump administration big win with rulings on key immigration cases – live
clusters.neutral_headline: Supreme court lets Trump turn back asylum seekers at US-Mexico border
bullet_summary: The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration can move forward with its efforts to strip more than 356,000 Syrian and Haitian immigrants of temporary protections
Reported by Guardian World, CBS News, BBC World and others

items.title: Supreme court hands Trump administration big win with rulings on key immigration cases – live
items.standfirst: Justices allow Trump administration to end protections for Haitians and Syrians and pave way to turn back asylum seekers at southern borderSupreme court lets Trump administration end TPS for Haitians and SyriansTrump officials can turn back asylum seekers at US-Mexico borderSign up for the Breaking News US emailHere’s my colleague Maanvi Singh’s report on the supreme court’s decision to give the Trump administration a green light to turn back asylum seekers at the US-Mexico border, in a ruling that fundamentally reshapes the US asylum system and concludes a battle that has spanned three administrations.In a major ruling, the supreme court has allowed the Trump administration to end legal protections for migrants fleeing violence and natural disaster in Haiti and Syria, exposing hundreds of thousands more people to potential deportation. Continue reading...
items.content: Justices allow Trump administration to end protections for Haitians and Syrians and pave way to turn back asylum seekers at southern borderSupreme court lets Trump administration end TPS for Haitians and SyriansTrump officials can turn back asylum seekers at US-Mexico borderSign up for the Breaking News US emailHere’s my colleague Maanvi Singh’s report on the supreme court’s decision to give the Trump administration a green light to turn back asylum seekers at the US-Mexico border, in a ruling that fundamentally reshapes the US asylum system and concludes a battle that has spanned three administrations.In a major ruling, the supreme court has allowed the Trump administration to end legal protections for migrants fleeing violence and natural disaster in Haiti and Syria, exposing hundreds of thousands more people to potential deportation. Continue reading...

items.title: Supreme Court lets Trump strip deportation protections from Syrians, Haitians
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration can move forward with its efforts to strip more than 356,000 Syrian and Haitian immigrants of temporary protections.
items.content: The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration can move forward with its efforts to strip more than 356,000 Syrian and Haitian immigrants of temporary protections.

items.title: Supreme court lets Trump turn back asylum seekers at US-Mexico border
items.standfirst: Decision allows Trump administration to block migrants from entering US soil and the right to claim asylumUS politics live – latest updatesSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe supreme court has given the Trump administration a green light to block asylum seekers at the US-Mexico border, in a decision that fundamentally reshapes the US asylum system.The decision allows the Trump administration to revive its so-called turn-back or “metering” policy, allowing federal agents at the US border to stop migrants from physically setting foot on US soil, where federal law guarantees them the right to claim asylum and protection from persecution. Continue reading...
items.content: Decision allows Trump administration to block migrants from entering US soil and the right to claim asylumUS politics live – latest updatesSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe supreme court has given the Trump administration a green light to block asylum seekers at the US-Mexico border, in a decision that fundamentally reshapes the US asylum system.The decision allows the Trump administration to revive its so-called turn-back or “metering” policy, allowing federal agents at the US border to stop migrants from physically setting foot on US soil, where federal law guarantees them the right to claim asylum and protection from persecution. Continue reading...

items.title: Supreme court conservatives accused of advancing ‘white-supremacist agenda’
items.standfirst: Lawmakers and advocates condemn ‘disastrous’ decisions that allow Trump officials to strip away migrant protectionsSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailLawmakers and immigration advocacy groups on Thursday sharply denounced two US supreme court rulings that allowed the Trump administration to strip certain immigration protections and fundamentally reshape the asylum system.Dozens of groups, advocates and members of Congress called the court’s decisions “disastrous” and “cruel”, while the Trump administration, Republican lawmakers and anti-immigrant groups celebrated the rulings. Continue reading...
items.content: Lawmakers and advocates condemn ‘disastrous’ decisions that allow Trump officials to strip away migrant protectionsSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailLawmakers and immigration advocacy groups on Thursday sharply denounced two US supreme court rulings that allowed the Trump administration to strip certain immigration protections and fundamentally reshape the asylum system.Dozens of groups, advocates and members of Congress called the court’s decisions “disastrous” and “cruel”, while the Trump administration, Republican lawmakers and anti-immigrant groups celebrated the rulings. Continue reading...

items.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
items.standfirst: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.
items.content: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.

items.title: Supreme Court allows Trump administration to lift deportation protections for Haitians, Syrians
items.standfirst: On Thursday, the predominantly Republican US Supreme Court authorised President Donald Trump's administration to revoke, without judicial review, a temporary status that protects 350,000 Haitian immigrants and 6,000 Syrians from deportation.
items.content: On Thursday, the predominantly Republican US Supreme Court authorised President Donald Trump's administration to revoke, without judicial review, a temporary status that protects 350,000 Haitian immigrants and 6,000 Syrians from deportation.

items.title: Trump can begin deportations of Syrian, Haitian TPS holders, Supreme Court says
items.standfirst: Writing for the court majority, Justice Samuel Alito that under the TPS law, the president has unreviewable authority to end the program, without intervention from the courts.
items.content: Writing for the court majority, Justice Samuel Alito that under the TPS law, the president has unreviewable authority to end the program, without intervention from the courts. (Image credit: Mandel Ngan)

I came here riding my motorbike as fast as I could,” said José Morillo, as he arrived outside a block of flats called Residencias Obelisco

items.title: Video captures moment of Venezuela earthquake
items.standfirst: Thousands are feared dead in the rubble of a devastated Venezuela on Thursday as rescue teams claw through the aftermath of two earthquakes. Lilia Luciano reports, and Rob Marciano looks into what caused the double-earthquake.
items.content: Thousands are feared dead in the rubble of a devastated Venezuela on Thursday as rescue teams claw through the aftermath of two earthquakes. Lilia Luciano reports, and Rob Marciano looks into what caused the double-earthquake.

items.title: Search for survivors underway as earthquake in Venezuela puts strain on resources

items.content: Back-to-back earthquakes hit Venezuela, killing at least 188 people and injuring thousands. NBC News' Ana Vanessa Herrero reports from Caracas about the search for survivors.

items.content: Venezuelans battle to rescue loved ones trapped alive beneath rubble of collapsed buildings after two major earthquakes.

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes: ‘intensive’ search for survivors as death toll rises to 188 – as it happened
items.standfirst: Delcy Rodríguez said authorities were shifting rescue teams from other parts of the country to the hardest-hit La Guaira areaVenezuela declares state of emergency after deadly twin earthquakesHave you been affected by the earthquakes in Venezuela?Volunteers, medics and relatives of victims have raced to the Altamira area in Caracas hoping to help save survivors from the rubble of collapsed buildings there.“I live far away [but] ... I came here riding my motorbike as fast as I could,” said José Morillo, as he arrived outside a block of flats called Residencias Obelisco. Continue reading...

items.title: NBC News reporter in Venezuela says people she knows might be missing under rubble
items.standfirst: Ana Vanessa Herrero, reporting for NBC News in Venezuela, sheds light on the hundreds of people looking for loved ones who might be missing under the rubble after two major earthquakes hit near the country's capital on Wednesday.
items.content: Ana Vanessa Herrero, reporting for NBC News in Venezuela, sheds light on the hundreds of people looking for loved ones who might be missing under the rubble after two major earthquakes hit near the country's capital on Wednesday.

items.title: Rescue teams race to Venezuela amid fears thousands killed in earthquakes
items.standfirst: US among countries sending help to search for survivors on north coast, where dozens of buildings flattenedRescue teams are racing to Venezuela’s shattered northern coast after almost simultaneous earthquakes reduced dozens of buildings to rubble, with thousands of people feared dead.The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, said the defence department would help search and rescue teams deploy to the affected region after Venezuela’s main gateway, the Simón Bolívar international airport, near the capital, Caracas, was badly damaged by 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes less than 40 seconds apart, late on Wednesday afternoon. Continue reading...
items.content: US among countries sending help to search for survivors on north coast, where dozens of buildings flattenedRescue teams are racing to Venezuela’s shattered northern coast after almost simultaneous earthquakes reduced dozens of buildings to rubble, with thousands of people feared dead.The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, said the defence department would help search and rescue teams deploy to the affected region after Venezuela’s main gateway, the Simón Bolívar international airport, near the capital, Caracas, was badly damaged by 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes less than 40 seconds apart, late on Wednesday afternoon. Continue reading...

items.title: US, Cuba and Iran join global rescue effort after Venezuela earthquakes
items.standfirst: Latin American and European countries also offer solidarity and help to nation already enduring a humanitarian crisisCountries around the world, including Iran, the US and Cuba, have committed to help with rescue efforts in Venezuela after deadly twin earthquakes hit the country.On the evening of 24 June, Venezuela was rocked by two back-to-back earthquakes with a magnitude of 7.2 and 7.5, which killed at least 164 people, injured close to 1,000 more, and caused significant damage to the capital, Caracas, as well as areas across the north of the country. Continue reading...
items.content: Latin American and European countries also offer solidarity and help to nation already enduring a humanitarian crisisCountries around the world, including Iran, the US and Cuba, have committed to help with rescue efforts in Venezuela after deadly twin earthquakes hit the country.On the evening of 24 June, Venezuela was rocked by two back-to-back earthquakes with a magnitude of 7.2 and 7.5, which killed at least 164 people, injured close to 1,000 more, and caused significant damage to the capital, Caracas, as well as areas across the north of the country. Continue reading...

Continue reading...
items.content: Rescue efforts under way after buildings reduced to rubble in capital and along northern coastHundreds of people are feared to have died and thousands have been injured in Venezuela’s largest earthquake in more than a century.Two earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 hit 39 seconds apart near the town of Morón. Continue reading...

items.content: Venezuelans searched for survivors beneath collapsed buildings Thursday and rescue teams raced to the areas hardest hit by a pair of powerful earthquakes that rocked northern Venezuela, killing at least 164 people with many more feared dead.

items.standfirst: On June 25, 2024, Kenyan protesters stormed #parliament amid #youth protests against economic #hardship and #corruption. Since, an annual #march is organised on this day to remember those killed during demonstrations, as frustration with #president William #Ruto grows.

clusters.title: Venezuela’s Powerful Earthquakes Were a Rare ‘Seismic Doublet’
clusters.neutral_headline: Venezuela’s Powerful Earthquakes Were a Rare ‘Seismic Doublet’
bullet_summary: Reported by Wired, NBC World

items.title: Venezuela’s Powerful Earthquakes Were a Rare ‘Seismic Doublet’
items.standfirst: The country was hit hard by a pair of quakes that happened in quick succession and were likely driven by stress being transferred from one part of the fault that runs through the country to another.
items.content: The country was hit hard by a pair of quakes that happened in quick succession and were likely driven by stress being transferred from one part of the fault that runs through the country to another.

items.title: What to know about ‘doublet’ earthquakes like the destructive pair that hit Venezuela
items.standfirst: Venezuela’s pair of earthquakes is considered doublet sequence. It’s not rare to have two quakes in short succession, but in this case, that likely led to more destruction.
items.content: Venezuela’s pair of earthquakes is considered doublet sequence. It’s not rare to have two quakes in short succession, but in this case, that likely led to more destruction.

clusters.title: Trump's 'Alligator Alcatraz' detention center closes after a year of controversy
clusters.neutral_headline: DeSantis boasts of deporting 21,000 as notorious Alligator Alcatraz jail closes
bullet_summary: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis said the Everglades facility had emptied after fulfilling its emergency role despite criticism over detainees' conditions and due process
Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: Trump's 'Alligator Alcatraz' detention center closes after a year of controversy
items.standfirst: Florida's controversial "Alligator Alcatraz" immigration detention centre, a symbol of US President Donald Trump's deportation drive, has closed after operating for less than a year, officials said on Thursday. Florida Governor Ron DeSantis said the Everglades facility had emptied after fulfilling its emergency role despite criticism over detainees' conditions and due process.
items.content: Florida's controversial "Alligator Alcatraz" immigration detention centre, a symbol of US President Donald Trump's deportation drive, has closed after operating for less than a year, officials said on Thursday. Florida Governor Ron DeSantis said the Everglades facility had emptied after fulfilling its emergency role despite criticism over detainees' conditions and due process.

items.title: DeSantis boasts of deporting 21,000 as notorious Alligator Alcatraz jail closes
items.standfirst: Florida immigration jail that became byword for cruelty and cost state taxpayers $1.2m a day shuts down after a yearRon DeSantis, Florida’s Republican governor, boasted on Thursday of deporting 21,000 people from Alligator Alcatraz, as he confirmed the closure of the notorious immigration jail hastily erected in the Everglades that became a byword for cruelty, human rights abuses and environmental damage.Standing beside Tom Homan, Donald Trump’s so-called border czar, at a press conference at the now dismantled site in Ochopee, in the environmentally sensitive region in south Florida, DeSantis presented its year-long operation as a victory for the president’s aggressive immigration enforcement agenda. Continue reading...
items.content: Florida immigration jail that became byword for cruelty and cost state taxpayers $1.2m a day shuts down after a yearRon DeSantis, Florida’s Republican governor, boasted on Thursday of deporting 21,000 people from Alligator Alcatraz, as he confirmed the closure of the notorious immigration jail hastily erected in the Everglades that became a byword for cruelty, human rights abuses and environmental damage.Standing beside Tom Homan, Donald Trump’s so-called border czar, at a press conference at the now dismantled site in Ochopee, in the environmentally sensitive region in south Florida, DeSantis presented its year-long operation as a victory for the president’s aggressive immigration enforcement agenda. Continue reading...

items.title: Home Office plan to use more military bases to house asylum seekers
items.standfirst: Three new sites in Oxfordshire, Suffolk, and Yorkshire could house about 3,750 asylum seekers if permission is granted.
items.content: Three new sites in Oxfordshire, Suffolk, and Yorkshire could house about 3,750 asylum seekers if permission is granted.

items.title: Charities condemn ‘arrogant’ plans to house asylum seekers at former military sites
items.standfirst: Planning permission has been sought for three additional military sites Home Office plans to use three more former military sites to house thousands of asylum seekers have been condemned as “arrogant”, “costly” and “a political fix” by refugee charities and local stakeholders.Planning permission is being sought to build “basic” accommodation at MOD Bicester in Oxfordshire, RAF Barnham in Suffolk and RAF Linton-on-Ouse in North Yorkshire, a statement said. These new sites could house 3,750 claimants, the government has claimed. Continue reading...
items.content: Planning permission has been sought for three additional military sites Home Office plans to use three more former military sites to house thousands of asylum seekers have been condemned as “arrogant”, “costly” and “a political fix” by refugee charities and local stakeholders.Planning permission is being sought to build “basic” accommodation at MOD Bicester in Oxfordshire, RAF Barnham in Suffolk and RAF Linton-on-Ouse in North Yorkshire, a statement said. These new sites could house 3,750 claimants, the government has claimed. Continue reading...

items.title: Philippines blocks GoreBox gaming app after school shooting kills three
items.standfirst: Investigators says one of two teenagers who allegedly opened fire on students at high school was regular playerPhilippine authorities have temporarily blocked the online gaming app GoreBox days after a rare school shooting in the south-east Asian country killed three students and injured 20 others.Investigators said that one of the two teenagers accused of opening fire on students at San Jose National high school in Tacloban city had regularly played the game, which allows players to use various weapons and depicts graphic violence. Continue reading...
items.content: Investigators says one of two teenagers who allegedly opened fire on students at high school was regular playerPhilippine authorities have temporarily blocked the online gaming app GoreBox days after a rare school shooting in the south-east Asian country killed three students and injured 20 others.Investigators said that one of the two teenagers accused of opening fire on students at San Jose National high school in Tacloban city had regularly played the game, which allows players to use various weapons and depicts graphic violence. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump says he’s ordered investigation into oil companies over alleged price gouging
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump accuses oil companies of gas price ‘gouging,’ calls for DOJ probe
bullet_summary: “The big Oil Companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for Oil,” the president said
Reported by Guardian Business, NBC World

items.title: Trump says he’s ordered investigation into oil companies over alleged price gouging
items.standfirst: Trump says he instructed justice department to investigate oil firms over high gas prices amid Middle East conflictDonald Trump said on Wednesday that he had instructed the US Department of Justice to investigate oil companies for alleged price gouging, accusing them of not lowering gas prices enough amid conflict in the Middle East.“The big oil companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for oil. Those prices are dropping like a rock! In other words, customers are being ‘gouged.’ I have instructed the DOJ to immediately start looking into this,” Trump wrote in a social media post late on Tuesday night. “Gasoline prices better start going down a lot faster than what I’m seeing!” Continue reading...

Those prices are dropping like a rock! In other words, customers are being ‘gouged.’ I have instructed the DOJ to immediately start looking into this,” Trump wrote in a social media post late on Tuesday night. “Gasoline prices better start going down a lot faster than what I’m seeing!” Continue reading...
